mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the subsequent month. This is an always-on trend signal based on demand-side momentum.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month rank IC between RSAFS YoY% and forward 20-day SPX returns is < 0.02, or the signal produces negative average returns in expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate to below 4.0% after a period above 4.5% signals an overheated labor market that will force the Fed to tighten, compressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% after being above 4.5% within the previous 6 months
failure_condition: When UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases over the next 10 years.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of strong retail sales growth and a low federal funds rate indicates a Goldilocks economy where consumer demand is robust and monetary policy remains accommodative, boosting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative more often than positive (less than 50% positive rate) over any 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion phase, rising industrial production signals capacity constraints and input cost pressures that erode profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In periods where INDPRO > 110, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth directly boosts consumer income and confidence, driving short-term bullish sentiment and positive 5-day equity returns following the monthly employment report.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward SPX return following months with above-median PAYEMS changes is not significantly positive (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, it indicates restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and slows economic activity, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: While FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of occurrences (indicating no consistent negative tilt).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial output and decline in retail sales suggests inventory accumulation without final demand, a classic signal of overproduction that leads to profit warnings and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% and RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0%
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 45% of cases, or the average excess return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts rise above 2,200 thousand units, it signals that the housing cycle is peaking and future demand will soften, leading to negative equity returns over the next month as construction activity decelerates.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns after HOUST exceeds 2200 are positive more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero.
